data.title: Constellium targets $780M–$820M adjusted EBITDA in 2026 as Vision 2028 launches
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Constellium SE (CSTM) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Ingrid Joerg highlighted an 11% increase in shipments to 365,000 tons and a 28% rise in revenue to $2.2 billion for the quarter, citing "higher shipments in each of our operating segments" and improved revenue per ton, including higher metal prices.
  • Joerg underscored a net income of $113 million for the quarter, a turnaround from a net loss of $47 million in the previous year, attributing this to "higher gross profit in the quarter versus last year." Adjusted EBITDA climbed to $280 million, including a noncash metal price lag benefit of $67 million. Excluding this, adjusted EBITDA reached $213 million, described as "a new fourth quarter record for us and is up 113% versus the $100 million in the fourth quarter last year."
  • Free cash flow was reported at $110 million for the quarter, with $40 million returned to shareholders through share repurchases. For the full year, shipments increased 4% to 1.5 million tons, revenue grew 15% to $8.4 billion, and net income reached $275 million. The company repurchased 8.9 million shares for $115 million and reduced leverage to 2.5x by year-end.
  • Joerg stated, "We delivered strong execution and demonstrated our ability to control costs throughout the year in 2025, and we believe we are well positioned heading into 2026 to capitalize on market opportunities as they arise."
  • CFO Jack Guo reported that the A&T segment saw adjusted EBITDA rise to $83 million, up 43% year-over-year, driven by "higher TID shipments," which increased 41%. The PARP segment achieved a quarterly record with adjusted EBITDA of $136 million (up 143%), led by 15% growth in packaging shipments. AS&I segment adjusted EBITDA reached $5 million, up $1 million, with industrial shipments rising 33% but automotive shipments declining 10%.
  • Guo added, "We are pleased today to announce our next group-wide excellence program, which we're calling Vision 2028. This program will target both operational efficiencies and cost reduction across our businesses and is one of the building blocks in our road map to our 2028 targets."

Outlook

  • Joerg announced, "Based on our current outlook, for 2026, we are targeting adjusted EBITDA, excluding the noncash impact of metal price lag in the range of $780 million to $820 million and free cash flow in excess of $200 million."
  • Guidance assumes "the recent demand trends in our end markets... will continue into at least the early part of 2026 and the overall macroeconomic environment to remain relatively stable."
  • The company reaffirmed its 2028 targets: adjusted EBITDA (excluding metal price lag) of $900 million and free cash flow of $300 million.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for the quarter was $2.2 billion, up 28% year-over-year, and net income reached $113 million, compared to a loss of $47 million the prior year. Adjusted EBITDA (excluding metal price lag) was $213 million for the quarter, a new record, and free cash flow stood at $110 million.
  • For the full year, revenue totaled $8.4 billion, net income was $275 million, and adjusted EBITDA (excluding metal price lag) reached $720 million. The company reduced leverage to 2.5x and maintained liquidity at $866 million.
  • Share repurchases amounted to $40 million in the quarter and $115 million for the year.

Q&A

  • Katja Jancic, BMO Capital Markets: Asked about the benefit of scrap spreads in the 2026 guidance. Guo explained, "Our expectation is given our scrap consumption needs are fully contracted in the first quarter, we should see similar type of benefits as we've seen in the fourth quarter of 2025."
  • Jancic followed up on whether the $900 million 2028 target could be conservative if scrap spreads remain high. Joerg responded that assumptions are "more conservative on the metal side" due to market volatility: "We don't know if the current situation... are going to last and for how long they are going to last."
  • William Peterson, JPMorgan: Inquired about factors beyond scrap spread for 2026 guidance. Joerg highlighted stable packaging demand, mixed automotive outlook, and steady aerospace business, with visibility improving for 2026 and 2027. She noted, "Military jets and space is going to continue to grow and be positive for us."
  • Timna Tanners, Wells Fargo: Asked about the second half cadence and Midwest premium assumptions. Guo clarified, "We have more certainty, more visibility... in the first half of the year... the incremental benefits on the metal side, you would expect it to kind of to be more significant in the first half of 2026."
  • Tanners also questioned potential material substitution in automotive. Joerg stated, "We really do not see any evidence of that... The trend to lightweight, the trend -- the better safety needs and the fuel economy, this is just going to stay."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for quantification of scrap spread impacts and guidance assumptions, frequently requesting specifics on market factors and cadence, signaling a slightly cautious but interested tone.
  • Management maintained a confident and detail-oriented approach, with Joerg stating, "We are extremely well positioned for the long-term success and remain focused on executing our strategy and shareholder value creation." Guo provided extended explanations on scrap and market dynamics, indicating confidence but also caution regarding volatility.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone was more assertive about the company’s ability to deliver against new targets and to manage cost structures amid complex market conditions, while analysts remained focused on potential variable factors affecting results.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for adjusted EBITDA (excluding metal price lag) has increased from a prior range of $670 million–$690 million (Q3) to $780 million–$820 million for 2026, indicating improved business expectations.
  • Management’s tone shifted from cautious optimism to stronger confidence, with the announcement of the Vision 2028 program as a new cost and operational efficiency driver.
  • Analysts continued to focus on scrap spreads, market cadence, and potential headwinds, but management’s responses included more specifics on operational initiatives and market breakdowns.
  • Key metrics such as revenue, net income, adjusted EBITDA, and free cash flow all showed substantial improvement over the previous quarter, while leverage was reduced from 3.1x to 2.5x.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted ongoing inflationary pressures across labor, energy, maintenance, and supplies, though "at more normal levels."
  • Tariffs continue to introduce market uncertainty, but Guo stated, "We believe at this stage, our direct tariff exposure remains manageable and the current tariff and trade policies are net positive for us."
  • Analyst questions highlighted concerns about the sustainability of metal price benefits, the impact of potential tariff relief, and the risk of market volatility.

Final Takeaway

Constellium finished 2025 with record operational and financial results, improved cost control, and robust free cash flow. Management set ambitious targets for 2026, including adjusted EBITDA of $780 million–$820 million and free cash flow above $200 million, supported by the newly announced Vision 2028 program focused on operational excellence and cost reduction. The company remains optimistic about its positioning in aerospace, packaging, and automotive markets and is committed to maintaining financial discipline and shareholder returns as it navigates dynamic market conditions.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Tpg re finance trust targets 3.5–3.75x leverage and expands multifamily, industrial exposure through $1.9B in new loans
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: TPG RE Finance Trust (TRTX) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Doug Bouquard reported, "2025 was an important turning point for TRTX. We closed $1.9 billion of new investments, drove 25% year-over-year growth in earning assets and generated distributable earnings of $0.97 per share, which outearned our dividend for the year." He noted, "we were able to achieve this while maintaining stable risk ratings, further diversifying our liability structure and ending the year with a 100% performing loan portfolio."
  • Bouquard highlighted a significant portfolio shift: "at the beginning of 2022, 30% of our balance sheet was exposed to multifamily and industrial collateral, whereas today, we have increased our combined exposure to those sectors to over 72%." He cited the closing of $927 million of new loans in Q4, with 62% multifamily and 38% industrial collateral, and emphasized, "over 90% of our new originations were with repeat borrowers."
  • The CEO outlined ongoing strategies: "we plan to continue to pull the many levers for growth at our disposal, which include continued net asset growth through prudent investment and risk management, increasing our leverage ratio towards our target of full investment and utilizing untapped liquidity."
  • Brandon Fox, Chief Accounting Officer & Interim CFO, stated, "For the fourth quarter of 2025, TRTX reported GAAP net income of $0.2 million. Distributable earnings for the quarter was $18.5 million or $0.24 per common share." He added, "Book value per common share decreased quarter-over-quarter to $11.07 from $11.25."

Outlook

  • Bouquard expressed a positive near-term view: "the combination of increased dry powder, a 10-year treasury hovering just above 4% and favorable real estate fundamentals should be drivers of continued growth and investment activity for TRTX."
  • He commented on origination trends: "our pipeline is incredibly robust. We are seeing a lot of activity really across all property types, all regions, a number of our borrowers, including repeat borrowers have been very active."
  • Regarding leverage, Bouquard noted, "we're really kind of targeting that 3.5 to 3.75:1 range is a target. I think that once we get to that point is where we will likely reassess, but I feel like that kind of gets us to what I would describe as close to fully invested."
  • On sector allocation, Bouquard indicated plans for further growth in industrial exposure: "it's probably somewhere in that kind of 25% to 30% range would be an area where I think that we perhaps touch the brakes a little bit."

Financial Results

  • Fox reported, "For the full year ending December 31, 2025, TRTX reported GAAP net income of $45.5 million or $0.57 per share and distributable earnings of $76.8 million or $0.97 per common share, a 1.01x coverage ratio of our annual dividend of $0.96 per share."
  • He shared, "Year-over-year, we grew our net assets from $3.3 billion to $4.1 billion or 25%. At year-end, our loan portfolio was 100% performing. Our weighted average risk rating for the loan portfolio is unchanged at 3.0."
  • The liability structure is now "82% non-mark-to-market, an increase of 6% from 77% at December 31, 2024. Year-over-year, our cost of funds declined 18 basis points or 9% from 2.0% to 1.82%."
  • Total leverage increased quarter-over-quarter to 3.02x from 2.64x due to substantial loan origination, and near-term liquidity stood at $143 million at year-end.

Q&A

  • Christopher Muller, Citizens JMP Securities, LLC, Research Division: "So it looks like only one close -- loan closed so far in the first quarter. Do you guys expect originations to slow a little bit in the first quarter? Or will it be more just like later closings past February? And how are you guys thinking about the pace of originations in 2026?" Bouquard answered that the pipeline remains strong with continued robust activity across all property types and regions, attributing this to many 5-year loans coming due and a clearer rate environment.
  • Muller followed up on loan spreads: "it looks like spreads on new loans is about 50 basis points below the portfolio average. Do you expect that type of drop-off on spreads for new loans to continue?" Bouquard said spreads were tighter but cost of funds had also moved in line, maintaining stable ROEs due to strong demand and aggressive back leverage.
  • Gabriel Poggi, Raymond James: "Can you just talk about target leverage again?...And any color you can provide on the REO assets at TRTX at the asset level and how you're thinking about those assets as the year progresses, especially if transaction volume picks up?" Bouquard stated the current leverage target is 3.5 to 3.75:1, with further REO progress expected this year as transaction volume increases.
  • Richard Shane, JPMorgan: "On ROE, your returns right now are about, call it, SOFR plus 5%. I'm curious when you think about the business model long term, what do you think the appropriate ROE target is as a function of a spread to SOFR?" Bouquard replied that 500 basis points above SOFR is an appropriate long-term target and noted the relevance of nonbank lending and regulatory capital trends.
  • Shane further asked about closing the value gap with the stock trading at a 20% discount to book. Bouquard responded that maximizing shareholder value is a clear focus and that TPG is consistently evaluating opportunities for both organic and inorganic growth.
  • John Nickodemus, BTIG: "Industrial exposure. I noticed that's gone up a bunch...How are you thinking about target levels for industrial? Should we expect to see that continue to rise?" Bouquard said industrial exposure could grow to 25–30%, after which the company would reassess.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst questions were focused on origination pace, loan spreads, leverage targets, the REO portfolio, and closing the stock's value gap, with a tone that was neutral and fact-seeking.
  • Management maintained a confident and positive tone in both prepared remarks and responses, repeatedly citing robust pipelines, stable credit performance, and strong lender relationships, with Bouquard stating, "we are confident in our ability to deliver continued strong performance."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management retained a constructive tone, but management's confidence level was higher, with more emphasis on growth and achievement of strategic goals.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • TRTX’s investment activity accelerated significantly, with Q4 originations ($927 million) far surpassing Q3 originations ($279 million).
  • Full-year GAAP net income was $45.5 million, up from a Q3 year-to-date total that was not explicitly given but would have been lower.
  • Book value per share decreased to $11.07 from $11.25 in Q3.
  • The liability structure shifted further toward non-mark-to-market funding (82% vs. 87% in Q3), and cost of funds continued to decline.
  • Management's strategic focus shifted more assertively toward increasing leverage, targeting 3.5–3.75x, and further growing multifamily and industrial exposure. Analyst questions in both quarters centered on growth, leverage, and closing the value gap, but Q4 featured greater management confidence in scaling the business.

Risks and Concerns

  • Fox reported a slight increase in the CECL reserve to 180 basis points from 176, and a downgrade of one multifamily loan due to operational challenges, representing about 1% of total loan commitments.
  • Bouquard acknowledged market competition was leading to tighter loan spreads but said that lower cost of funds was mitigating the impact on returns.
  • Analyst concerns included the sustainability of origination pace, the effect of lower loan spreads, and the ability to close the stock's discount to book value. Management emphasized robust pipelines, prudent risk management, and strong liquidity as mitigating factors.

Final Takeaway

TRTX closed 2025 with significant momentum, highlighted by a record $1.9 billion in new investments and a strategic repositioning toward multifamily and industrial assets, now comprising over 72% of the portfolio. Management is targeting a leverage ratio of 3.5–3.75x and expects continued robust origination activity, supported by a strong pipeline and favorable market dynamics. The company’s liability structure and liquidity remain solid, and all loans are performing. Management is focused on growth, risk management, and narrowing the stock’s discount to book value through both organic and inorganic strategies, entering 2026 with confidence and a clear path to further value creation for shareholders.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Crude oil and precious metals jump as U.S.-Iran tensions rise, Russia-Ukraine talks break down
data.description:
U.S. crude oil futures surged 4% Wednesday, while gold and silver prices resumed their upward flight, on persistent worries about a potential conflict between the U.S. and Iran, and after peace talks between Russia and Ukraine collapsed after only two hours.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for March delivery +4.1% to $64.88/bbl, front-month Brent crude (CO1:COM) for April delivery +3.8% to $69.98/bbl, Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for April delivery +2.2% to $5,017/oz, and Comex March silver (XAGUSD:CUR) +6% at $78/oz.

Iran's foreign minister said Tuesday that nuclear talks in Geneva were "constructive," which helped to drag down oil prices, but Vice President Vance said Iran has not addressed U.S. red lines in the discussions and that President Trump reserves the right to use military force.

"We do have a very powerful military, and the president has shown a willingness to use it," Vance told Fox News in an interview.

Adding to the jittery mood among traders, Axios reported that a U.S. military campaign against Iran likely would be "a massive, weeks-long campaign that would look more like a full-fledged war" than last month's raid that captured Venezuelan President Maduro.

The Trump administration is "closer to a major war in the Middle East than most Americans realize," which "could begin very soon," Axios said.

Iran is rushing to prepare for war by deploying forces, fortifying nuclear sites, and expanding domestic crackdowns, The Wall Street Journal reported.

Iran and Russia reportedly will conduct navy drills in the Sea of Oman and the northern Indian Ocean on Thursday, after Iran's Revolutionary Guards conducted military drills in the Strait of Hormuz earlier this week.

Meanwhile, peace talks between Russia and Ukraine in Geneva ended after only two hours, with Ukraine's President Zelenskiy accusing Russia of stalling.

"Neither of the geopolitical negotiations shows any tangible progress," Tamas Varga of oil broker PVM told Reuters, referring to Ukraine and Iran. "It is understandable that yesterday's dip was viewed as a buying opportunity."

ETFs: (USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Top 10 mid-cap energy stocks ranked based on their lowest RSI
data.description:
As we move into the second half of the quarter, below is a list of the top 10 mid-cap energy stocks ranked based on their lowest RSI. These U.S.-based energy companies have market capitalizations ranging from approximately $2 billion to $8 billion.

The list is topped by Comstock Resources (CRK), with an RSI of 34. Centrus Energy (LEU) and CVR Energy (CVI) follow, with Core Natural Resources (CNR) and Peabody Energy (BTU) rounding out the top five. The RSI values across the top 10 range from 34 to 51, indicating these stocks are trading well below overbought levels.

These stocks represent various energy sub-sectors, including oil and gas exploration and production, coal and consumable fuels, oil and gas refining, and drilling services. Uranium Energy (UEC), Gulfport Energy (GPOR), and Solaris Energy Infrastructure (SEI) are also among the top-ranked stocks.

The Relative Strength Index is a momentum oscillator that measures the velocity and magnitude of price changes, plotting them on a scale from 0 to 100. RSI compares the magnitude of recent gains to recent losses over a chosen lookback period—typically 14 days. RSI readings of 70 or above are generally considered a signal that a stock may be overbought and potentially poised for a pullback.

Here is the list:

  1. Comstock Resources (CRK), RSI: 34

  2. Centrus Energy (LEU), RSI: 37

  3. CVR Energy (CVI), RSI: 40

  4. Core Natural Resources (CNR), RSI: 41

  5. Peabody Energy (BTU), RSI: 42

  6. Uranium Energy (UEC), RSI: 45

  7. Gulfport Energy (GPOR), RSI: 46

  8. Solaris Energy Infrastructure (SEI), RSI: 50

  9. Murphy Oil (MUR), RSI: 50

  10. Helmerich & Payne (HP), RSI: 51

Energy ETFs: (XLE), (AMLP), (VDE), (XOP), (OIH), and (IXC)

data.title: Etsy shares snap six-session losing streak ahead of results
data.description:
Etsy (ETSY) shares snapped six straight sessions of losses, as the stock was up 3.5% at $45.89 on Wednesday.

The global online marketplace fell 19.5% in the preceding six sessions. The stock is down 26% over the past one month and has dropped over 17% so far this year.

Etsy is scheduled to announce Q4 earnings results on Thursday, February 19th, before market open.

"Etsy continues to face declining engagement and flat Gross Merchandise Sales, despite incremental improvements and a new CEO at the helm," pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, ETSY has a Hold rating with a score of 2.9 out of 5. The company received an A- in the prospect of profitability, while it got a D in growth factor.

Turning to the Wall Street community, eight analysts gave ETSY a Buy and above rating. 22 analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, while two recommended Sell.

 
 
 

Seeking Alpha analysts are cautious and see the stock as a Hold.

data.title: American Tower snaps after six consecutive sessions of gains
data.description:
American Tower (NYSE:AMT) snapped a six-day winning streak, falling 2.35% to $188.56 in Wednesday afternoon trading.

Over the prior six sessions, the stock had climbed more than 12.74%, significantly outperforming the S&P 500 Index, which declined 1.28% during the same period. Year to date in 2025, American Tower shares are up over 7.63%, compared with a marginal 0.03% drop in the broader S&P 500.

Looking at Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating, AMT carries a Hold recommendation with a score of 2.60 out of 5. The stock received an A grade for profitability, a B for growth, and a D- for valuation, reflecting mixed fundamentals.

In contrast, Wall Street analysts, sentiment remains more constructive, with 16 analysts assigning a Strong Buy rating, while 7 recommend holding the shares.

Seeking Alpha analyst Justin Purohit noted that shares of leading telecommunications REIT American Tower are currently trading near the midpoint of their 52-week range, following a roughly 6% gain over the past month.

He added that the company’s operating performance should continue to benefit from the long-term secular tailwind of rising wireless data consumption and reiterated a Buy rating on the stock for investors looking for a core portfolio holding.

Seeking Alpha's own analysts are also positive on the stock, maintaining a Buy rating on AMT.

data.title: Insulet gains after Q4 print as outlook tops consensus
data.description:
Insulet (PODD) shares spiked on Wednesday after the insulin pump maker beat Street forecasts with its Q4 2025 results and issued an outlook that, according to Leerink Partners, exceeded consensus at the midpoint.

Its Q4 revenue jumped ~29% YoY on a constant currency basis to $783.8M, exceeding the consensus by $15.1M, as its revenue from its Omnipod insulin pump rose ~28% YoY and ~42% YoY in the U.S. and overseas markets to reach $567.8M and $214.0M, respectively.

Meanwhile, Insulet’s (PODD) adjusted diluted earnings per share climbed ~35% YoY to $1.55, beating the consensus by $0.09 as the company recorded ~19% of adjusted operating margin, indicating a 30 bps rise from the prior year period.

For 2025, PODD reported $4.97 of adjusted diluted EPS on $2.7B in revenue with ~54% YoY and ~30% YoY growth, respectively, with a 270 bps increase in adjusted operating margin.

The Acton, Massachusetts-based MedTech indicated 25% YoY - 27% YoY and 20% YoY - 22% YoY in forex-adjusted revenue growth for Q1 and 2026, respectively, alongside an adjusted EPS growth of more than 25% backed by 100 bps growth in adjusted operating margin. 

“The FY26 guide will be viewed positively by investors, supported by strong underlying growth both in the US and OUS,” Leerink analyst Mike Kratky, who has an Outperform rating on PODD, wrote, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: BofA breaks down performance metrics for top social media platforms
data.description:
Bank of America on Wednesday released a report analyzing performance metrics for social media platforms following the release of January Sensor Tower time spent data.

Average Revenue Per User Growth

BofA noted that Reddit (RDDT) saw the strongest ARPU growth in the U.S. for the fourth quarter at 53%, followed by Meta (META) at 22%, Snap (SNAP) at 13%, YouTube (GOOG) (GOOGL) at 5%, and Pinterest (PINS) at 5%.

In international markets, Reddit's ARPU growth was 39%, followed by Pinterest and Meta at 18% and YouTube and Snap at 9%.

"Reddit is benefiting from improving ad performance, plus 4Q ad load growth, which is expected to moderate in 1Q. Meta continues to lead in AI-driven monetization gains. Subscriptions aided Snap, while Pinterest US growth trailed," the research firm said.

Revenue Per Hour Growth

Reddit had the highest Q4 RPH growth in the U.S. at 92%, followed by Snap at 19%, Meta at 10%, YouTube at 1%, and Pinterest at -14%.

Internationally, Reddit's RPH growth was up 62%, followed by Pinterest at 29%, Snap at 21%, Meta at 17%, and YouTube at 13%.

"Reddit’s RPH growth driven by higher ad loads and improved conversion, while Pinterest’s RPH decline reflects solid usage growth and lagging monetization (tariff pressures, small SMB share). Meta remains best-in-class in AI-driven monetization. YouTube RPH does not include subscription revs, which are incremental," the research firm said.

Time Spent

For the month of January, Instagram's daily average time spent per active user in the U.S. was up 12%, followed by Pinterest at 10%, TikTok (BDNCE) at 7%, Facebook at 4%, YouTube at 2%, X at 2%, and Yelp (YELP) with flat growth. Reddit had the largest decline at -9%, followed by Snap at -4%.

Internationally, Yelp had the highest growth at 10%, followed by Instagram at 7%, Facebook at 3%, TikTok at 2%, and X at 1%. Snap had the largest decline at -7%, followed by Reddit at -6%, YouTube at -5%, and Pinterest at -3%.

"Larger & more advanced AI models enhancing content targeting & driving strong usage growth at Instagram. Reddit facing a mix shift toward newer users who are relatively less engaged and slower growth of logged-in users. Remain cautious on Snap declines," BofA said.

Users

For January, Pinterest had the highest growth in U.S. mobile DAUs at 13%, followed by TikTok at 5%, Instagram at 2%, and YouTube at 1%. Yelp had the largest decline in users, down 16%, followed by Reddit at -9%, X at -8%, Snap at -7%, and Facebook at -2%. 

Internationally, Pinterest had the highest growth at 10%, followed by Reddit at 10%, Instagram at 8%, TikTok at 6%, and Snap at 5%. X DAUs declined 9%, followed by Yelp at -2%, Facebook at -1%, and YouTube at -1%.

"Pinterest seeing improving engagement, aiding the DAU/MAU ratio. Reddit US DAUs flat m/m, while Int’l benefiting from machine translation and paid marketing. TikTok US DAU growth rates improving on easy comps and possibly safety initiatives, Int’l steady," the research firm said.

Mobile Download Trends

In the U.S./North America, TikTok had the highest growth in mobile downloads for January at 137%, followed by X at 17% and Yelp at flat. Reddit declined 36%, followed by Instagram at -22%, Snapchat at -20%, Facebook at -18%, Pinterest at -11%, and YouTube at -7%.

Internationally, X had the highest growth in mobile downloads at 18%, and Pinterest at 6%. YouTube downloads declined 24%, followed by Instagram at -19%, TikTok at -18%, Facebook at -16%, Snapchat at -8%, Reddit at -5%, and Yelp at -3%.

More on Reddit, Meta, etc.

data.title: Verisk signals $3.19B–$3.24B 2026 revenue target and launches $1.5B accelerated share repurchase following portfolio actions
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Verisk Analytics (VRSK) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Lee Shavel highlighted that Verisk delivered organic constant currency revenue growth of 6.6%, organic constant currency adjusted EBITDA growth of 8.5%, and strong free cash flow growth for 2025, stating these results were in line with earlier guidance. Shavel explained, "we continue to have confidence in delivering against our long-term growth targets based on the ongoing adoption of data and technology across the global insurance industry and our opportunity to support the needs of our clients and address their objectives with our distinct capabilities."

  • Shavel announced the termination of the agreement to acquire Acculynx, citing, "following the notice from the FTC that the review would be extended, the opportunity cost of waiting on the sidelines through a long, uncertain and costly approval process was too high given the rapidly evolving environment." He also noted the sale of Verisk Marketing Solutions, describing it as "a demonstration of our ongoing active portfolio management and our commitment to focusing on data, analytics and technology solutions for the global insurance industry."

  • The CEO introduced Steve Kauderer as the new head of Verisk’s claims business, emphasizing his experience in transforming insurance industry workflows with data and technology.

  • Shavel spotlighted new AI-powered product launches, including the next generation of AI-enabled estimating products, XactGen, and the continued rollout of XactXpert and XactAI, highlighting widespread industry adoption and client enthusiasm.

  • CFO Elizabeth Mann reported, "on a consolidated and GAAP basis, fourth quarter revenue was $779 million, up 5.9% versus the prior year. Net income was $197 million, a 6.2% decrease versus the prior year, while diluted GAAP earnings per share were $1.42, down 1% versus the prior year."

  • Mann explained, "subscription revenues, which comprised 84% of our total revenues in the quarter, grew 7.7% on an OCC basis, compounding from the 11% organic constant currency increase that we delivered in the fourth quarter of 2024."

Outlook

  • Mann guided that consolidated revenue for 2026 is expected to be in the range of $3.19 billion to $3.24 billion, with adjusted EBITDA between $1.79 billion and $1.83 billion and adjusted EBITDA margin of 56% to 56.5%. She stated, "this margin compares to the normalized baseline of 55.8% as reported margins in 2025 included a 40 basis point nonrecurring benefit from foreign currency translation."

  • Interest expense for 2026 is projected at $190 million to $200 million; capital expenditure is forecast at $260 million to $280 million; and the tax rate is expected to be 23% to 26%.

  • Adjusted earnings per share guidance is $7.45 to $7.75, with Mann noting, "the sale of Verisk Marketing Solutions presents an $0.11 headwind to EPS."

  • Management cautioned that Q1 2026 reported revenue will be lower than Q4 2025 by a low single-digit percentage due to the divestiture of Verisk Marketing Solutions, and that the first quarter is expected to be the trough for reported dollars and growth rate.

Financial Results

  • Mann reported fourth quarter OCC revenue growth of 5.2%, with 7.2% growth in underwriting and 0.5% in claims; for the full year 2025, OCC revenue growth was 6.6%. She cited that transactional revenues, comprising 16% of total revenues, declined 6.5% on an OCC basis in Q4, primarily due to lower volumes in Property Estimating Solutions and continued low levels of weather activity.

  • OCC adjusted EBITDA growth was 6.2% in the quarter, with total adjusted EBITDA margin at 56.1%, up 200 basis points from the prior year period. For full year 2025, OCC adjusted EBITDA grew 8.5% and adjusted EBITDA margins were 56.2%, up 150 basis points year-over-year.

  • Adjusted net income increased 11.3% to $253 million, and diluted adjusted EPS increased 13% for the quarter. Full year adjusted EPS was $7.16, up 7.8%.

  • Free cash flow for the quarter increased to $276 million; for the full year, free cash flow rose 30% to $1.19 billion.

  • The company returned $286 million through repurchases and dividends in the quarter and plans a $1.5 billion accelerated share repurchase, with board approval increasing share repurchase authorization to $2.5 billion in total.

Q&A

  • Toni Kaplan, Morgan Stanley, asked whether clients prefer to use Verisk’s data to build their own AI products or prefer Verisk to deliver AI solutions. Shavel replied that both approaches are present, depending on client size and sophistication, but "the important thing is our data is at the core because that analytics function relies on good quality industry-wide data."

  • Manav Patnaik, Barclays, inquired about the relationship between software and data. Shavel stated, "the predominance of our software footprint is related to that data delivery and integration function."

  • Faiza Alwy, Deutsche Bank, questioned pricing power with new technology rollouts and margin implications. Shavel indicated that "all of our businesses are fundamentally value-driven from a pricing standpoint... there’s inherent operating leverage associated with that."

  • Andrew Nicholas, William Blair, asked about the path to recovery for transactional revenue. Mann noted the main drop was due to storm comparisons, with other factors such as government contracts and subscription conversions, but expressed expectations that transactional revenue would be a source of strength over the long term.

  • Keen Fai Tong, Goldman Sachs, queried the limited margin expansion in 2026 guidance. Mann explained the normalized operational baseline and noted "guidance does show modest but meaningful margin expansion from there."

  • Other analyst questions centered on pricing feedback, competitive dynamics in auto, module adoption rates, and the impact of AI and data moats, with management providing detailed responses on each.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised multiple questions around the sustainability of pricing, margin dynamics, competitive pressures, AI strategy, and the recovery of transactional revenue, reflecting a slightly cautious but constructive tone, particularly regarding growth levers and the impact of recent divestitures.

  • Management maintained a confident and positive tone, frequently emphasizing the company’s value proposition, recurring revenue growth, and strategic execution. Phrases such as "we are confident," "we continue to have confidence," and "we are energized by the opportunity that lies ahead" were repeatedly used by Shavel and Mann.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, there was greater analyst focus on the implications of portfolio changes and margin sustainability, while management’s tone remained consistently optimistic, particularly about the opportunity in AI and the company’s financial resilience.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 marks an increase from previous quarter’s range, with explicit revenue and EBITDA targets, while the previous quarter’s guidance excluded the Acculynx transaction and was more cautious.

  • Major strategic shift is the termination of the Acculynx acquisition and the sale of Verisk Marketing Solutions, compared to last quarter’s anticipation of Acculynx’s integration.

  • Analyst focus evolved from M&A integration and AI competitive threats to operational execution, margin drivers, and capital allocation.

  • Key metric changes include growth in adjusted EBITDA margin and substantial increase in free cash flow, along with an accelerated share repurchase plan.

  • Management’s confidence level remained high, but with more detail about portfolio discipline and capital returns.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited temporary headwinds from low weather activity and a reduction in a government contract, each impacting revenue growth by approximately 1%.

  • Transactional revenues remain pressured by weather variability and softness in Personal Lines Auto.

  • Guidance flagged tougher comparisons in the first half of 2026, lower run rates in Property Estimating Solutions, and a work stoppage on a government contract as revenue headwinds.

  • Analyst concerns included competitive dynamics in auto, margin expansion, and the sustainability of price increases.

Final Takeaway

Verisk’s leadership emphasized its ability to deliver solid growth and profitability in 2025, highlighted by portfolio streamlining and continued innovation in AI-driven insurance analytics. The company set ambitious 2026 revenue and earnings targets, reinforced by robust free cash flow and a substantial share repurchase program. Management’s outlook remains confident, supported by a resilient business model, continued adoption of advanced technologies, and an active approach to capital allocation and portfolio management.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Wall Street pushes higher as tech regains momentum
data.description:
Wall Street’s major market averages pushed higher on Wednesday as the tech sector rebounded and regained momentum.

On the trading session, the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was last up by +1.3%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) added +0.9%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was up +0.7%.

From a sector vantage point, seven of the S&P 500’s 11 segments were in positive trading territory, with energy leading the charge. At the other end of the spectrum, utilities have suffered the most so far.

Treasury yields inched higher as the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) moved up 2 basis points to 3.46%, and the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) moved up by 2 basis points to 4.08%. 

On the economic front, durable goods orders fell less than expected in December, while housing starts and building permits rose in the same month. 

Additionally, industrial production figures increased more than expected in January, and the FOMC minutes will be released later in the day. 

As for stocks that were on the move, shares of Garmin (GRMN) climbed 9.1%, and shares of Palo Alto Networks (PANW) came down by 5.6%.
